title: wyred contract v0 — series-fork lifecycle record (.lifecycle.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the break_lock -> series-fork lifecycle record, emitted once (watchy_v1_revb, the fork of watchy_v1_reva). Source: wyred/emit.py fork path + `check_lock_violations`. There is NO prose (F1). Nine keys, all always emitted. F4: `forked_from` here is a bare STRING naming the parent artifact ("watchy_v1_reva") — NOT the {series, reason} object the l1/records artifacts use (common.defs.json#/$defs/forked_from_record). `parent_stamp`/`child_stamp` reuse the shared stamp $def. The three code arrays hold lock-gate codes: F12 records the closed-vocabulary decision (enum-constrained to the two codes `check_lock_violations` can emit). additionalProperties:false.

forked_from.description: F4: the PARENT artifact name as a bare string ("watchy_v1_reva"). Distinct from the {series, reason} object form of `forked_from` in l1/records.

new_series.description: the child's new series letter (free string; "B" in golden)

legal_fork_codes.description: codes from validating the legal fork against the parent baseline; [] means the fork is legal.

tamper_series_hand_edit_codes.description: codes the gate must raise when the fork record is stripped but the series is hand-edited (counter-probe): ["SERIES_UNJUSTIFIED"] in golden.

tamper_locked_edit_codes.description: codes the gate must raise when a locked parent decision is edited in place with no fork (counter-probe): ["LOCK_VIOLATION"] in golden.

ok.description: overall lifecycle verdict for this fork

lock_violation_code_array.description: An array of lock-gate codes. F12: the vocabulary is CLOSED to the two codes `wyred/emit.py::check_lock_violations` can emit — LOCK_VIOLATION and SERIES_UNJUSTIFIED — the sole gate producer of every code in legal_fork_codes/tamper_series_hand_edit_codes/tamper_locked_edit_codes. Duplicates are legal (one code per violating locked group). The only out-of-vocabulary string any producer can inject is the emit.py sentinel "(no locked entry to tamper)" into tamper_locked_edit_codes (used only when the parent has no locked entry to probe), which appears in NO golden; encoding it would be a silent widening (invariant 1), so it is excluded — a future lifecycle carrying it, or any third code, is a contract event.
